title: dryas octopetala subsp. oxyodonta
intro: Dryas octopetala subsp. oxyodonta, also known as the mountain avens, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the rose family that is native to the mountainous regions of the world. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its alkaloid and glycoside content. The medicinal properties of mountain avens have been reported to provide various health benefits, including wound healing, anti-arthritic, and anti-anxiety effects. In herbal preparations, mountain avens can be found in teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat various health conditions, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy.
